evaluations.reasoning: Standard ORDER BY injection with a trivial space-delimited keyword WAF. The bypass is straightforward (use comments or no-space syntax). Calibrated at 1.1 vs target 1.5, indicating the challenge is easier than intended. Clean Flask code but cookie-cutter logistics scenario.

Blind SQLi in a hidden endpoint revealed via robots.txt, with a single-pass keyword removal WAF (SELSELECTECT bypass). The discovery phase adds a layer, and the blind boolean extraction requires some skill. Decent calibration at 1.4.

Another ORDER BY injection with a regex keyword+whitespace WAF. Calibrated at 0.85 — significantly below target 1.5, meaning it's trivially easy. The debug endpoint name is discoverable but the overall challenge lacks depth.

Second-order SQLi through a saved region name is genuinely creative — the player stores a payload in the profile, then the dashboard query triggers it. The case-sensitive WAF (uppercase only) is the weakest link, but the two-step exploitation chain elevates this above most vanilla entries.

Blind SQLi in a compliance verification endpoint. The WAF blocks spaces and common keywords, requiring tab or comment-based bypasses. Reasonable difficulty but the scenario and vulnerability pattern are standard fare for AI-generated SQLi challenges.

Hidden API endpoint discovered via a static JS file, with a single-pass keyword stripping WAF. The red herring parameterized query in the inspections form is a nice touch, but the actual vulnerability is too easy to exploit. Calibrated at 0.85 — well below target.

ORDER BY injection with a base64 encoding layer adds a nice obfuscation step. Players must encode their payload before sending it, which is a genuine twist. Combined with a keyword blacklist and hidden credentials in HTML comments, this offers a reasonable multi-step challenge.

Second-order SQLi through a profile sector field with a case-sensitive WAF. The second-order pattern is creative, but the WAF bypass (just use lowercase) is trivially easy. Good code quality with Bootstrap and flash messages. Calibrated too low at 0.85.

The WAF only checks GET parameters, so switching to a POST JSON request completely bypasses it. This method-based bypass is a realistic misconfiguration. Good code quality with Bootstrap and proper structure, but the vulnerability is exploitable once you discover the POST method.

A debug mode that enables a raw WHERE clause injection. The help page literally tells you to use debug=true and the internal_metadata table name. The keyword+space WAF is trivially bypassed. Far too easy for a medium difficulty target — calibrated at 0.9.

The space-blocking WAF forces genuine skill — players must use tabs, parentheses, or other whitespace alternatives. Combined with a blind ORDER BY injection and a hidden diagnostics page with table name hints, this approaches medium difficulty well. Calibrated at 1.6, close to target.

Trivial case-sensitive WAF (only blocks uppercase) on an ORDER BY injection. Any lowercase variation bypasses it immediately. The robots.txt hint to the diagnostics endpoint is standard. Calibrated at 0.9 — too easy.

String injection in pod diagnostics with a keyword+whitespace WAF. The WAF can be bypassed by appending keywords without trailing spaces. Bootstrap code is clean but the challenge is cookie-cutter. Credentials in the footer make entry trivial.

Injection through a SQL enrichment clause (JOIN injection point) is genuinely creative and different from the standard ORDER BY pattern. The WAF blocks spaces and common keywords, requiring tab/newline bypasses for JOIN-based data extraction. API documentation page adds realism.

Search API with single-pass keyword stripping plus space stripping. The SELSELECTECT double-keyword bypass is well-known and mechanical. Calibrated at 0.85 — far below target. Nearly identical pattern to several other vanilla entries.

ORDER BY blind injection with keyword + space blocking. Calibrated at 2.1, actually harder than target. The unauthenticated endpoint and disabled login reduce realism. The challenge is technically sound but lacks creative elements beyond the standard WAF bypass.

ORDER BY injection with keyword + space blocking behind a multi-step discovery flow (credentials in HTML comment, manifest sorting). Clean Bootstrap UI with a tracking feature. Good calibration at 1.35. Solid but unremarkable execution of the standard pattern.

ORDER BY injection via POST JSON with a WAF that blocks union/sleep/benchmark but NOT select/from/where — a major oversight that makes exploitation trivial. The POST JSON interface is a nice touch but the WAF gaps undermine the challenge significantly.

Excellent second-order SQLi design — player creates a report with a malicious sort column that gets stored, then viewing the report triggers the injection. The two-step exploitation chain through a realistic report-building interface is the most creative pattern in this vanilla set. WAF allows enough for the stored payload to survive.

ORDER BY injection hidden behind cookie-based access control. Players must discover the admin cookie requirement from an HTML comment, set it, then exploit the sort parameter. The discovery chain adds value, but the WAF and injection are standard once you reach the endpoint.

Hidden API requiring a custom X-Pulse-Auth header discovered in JavaScript, with a single-pass keyword stripping WAF. The header discovery adds a step, but once past it, the injection is straightforward. Calibrated at 0.85.

The strictest WAF in the vanilla set — blocks spaces, tabs, newlines, carriage returns, and common keywords including 'join'. Players must craft payloads using only parentheses and non-standard characters. Combined with a hidden API discovered via JS file and a pharmaceutical cold-chain scenario, this is one of the stronger vanilla entries.

Hidden API in an HTML comment with keyword stripping + space removal. The SELSELECTECT double-keyword bypass combined with no-space techniques is standard but requires two bypass layers. The manifest verification endpoint is a plausible legacy API.

The best vanilla challenge — second-order injection through an X-Warehouse-Ref HTTP header stored via procurement requests, then triggered on an audit page requiring a staff cookie. Four distinct steps: discover robots.txt → set cookie → store header payload → view audit. The header-based injection vector is highly creative and realistic.

Standard ORDER BY injection with a keyword+space WAF. The bypass is straightforward using comments or alternative whitespace. Reports page with dropdown sorting is realistic but the challenge pattern is repeated many times across this set.

Word-boundary regex WAF is more robust than simple string matching — players must use SQL constructs that don't match word boundaries. Well-structured multi-page app with maintenance logs and driver fleet. Calibrated at 1.75, close to target.

ORDER BY injection with a keyword blacklist that misses SELECT, FROM, WHERE — making UNION-free extraction still possible with subqueries. Good code structure with error handling and carrier check API, but the challenge pattern is generic.

Second-order SQLi through field sort preferences — store a payload via settings, trigger it when viewing sensor logs. The keyword+space blocking WAF requires non-trivial bypass techniques for the stored payload. The agricultural IoT scenario with per-field settings is plausible and well-designed.

Blind SQLi in a hidden POST API discovered via a JavaScript comment. The WAF blocks keywords and spaces, requiring tab/newline bypasses. Calibrated at 2.25 — significantly harder than target 1.5, which hurts difficulty accuracy. The challenge is technically sound but overshoots the intended difficulty.

ORDER BY blind injection with space and keyword blocking behind session auth. Bootstrap UI with dropdown sorting is clean. Calibrated at 1.65, close to target. The WAF forces real bypass creativity but the overall pattern is familiar.

Numeric injection in a diagnostic API endpoint with space and keyword blocking. The dark-themed renewable energy UI is the best-looking in the vanilla set. The WAF requires genuine bypass skill with tab characters. Calibrated at 1.85, slightly above target but reasonable.

Perfectly calibrated at 1.5 — the only vanilla challenge to exactly match the target difficulty. ORDER BY injection with space, hash, and keyword blocking. Bootstrap UI with flash messages and compliance documentation. Solid execution of a standard pattern.

Injection through a datetime() function parameter is the most unusual injection point in the vanilla set — players must understand SQLite datetime parsing to craft payloads. The WAF misses many keywords, reducing the bypass challenge. Creative vulnerability location elevates an otherwise standard challenge.

ORDER BY injection requiring a custom X-FMS-Debug header discovered via an API documentation page. The multi-step discovery (docs → header → injection) is well-structured. Space and keyword blocking WAF requires tab bypasses. Good API docs template.

ORDER BY injection with a regex-based WAF that blocks all whitespace via \s+. Players must use parentheses-only payloads or URL-encoded alternatives. The industrial parts catalog is a realistic scenario. Clean code with proper teardown and error handling.

The WAF uses \s regex to block ALL whitespace characters and word-boundary matching for OR/AND. This is one of the hardest WAFs to bypass — requiring parentheses-only CASE/WHEN constructs. Combined with an HTML schema hint and no auth requirement, the exploitation demands real SQLi skill.

ORDER BY injection in a deep-sea drone telemetry API with space and keyword blocking. The dark nautical theme and drone monitoring interface are well-designed. Session auth and JS-based sorting UI add realism. Standard WAF bypass required.

Header injection through X-Backend-Cluster is a creative attack surface that differs from the typical parameter injection. The legacy archive discovery via HTML comments and the keyword+space WAF create a decent multi-step challenge. The cluster routing concept is realistic.

Numeric injection in a URL path parameter with a keyword blacklist WAF. The error page helpfully reveals debug info. The agricultural silo scenario is fine but the vulnerability is easy to discover and exploit once you test arithmetic operations.

ORDER BY injection with single-pass keyword stripping discovered via dashboard JavaScript. The SELSELECTECT bypass is mechanical and well-documented. Good aerospace theme and dashboard telemetry display, but the exploitation requires minimal skill.

The luxury estate booking theme is the most visually distinctive in the set — gold-on-black design feels genuinely different. POST JSON API for sorting with space and keyword blocking. The login system with bookings adds depth. Creative theming elevates standard mechanics.

The WAF explicitly allows /**/ comments as a bypass path — once discovered, exploitation is trivial. The legacy API discovery via JS comments is standard. Dark space theme is nice. The intentional WAF gap makes this easier than the calibrated 1.1 suggests.

ORDER BY injection in an internal API discovered via HTML comments. The word-boundary regex WAF explicitly allows CASE/WHEN — the intended bypass path. The secure parameterized search on the public endpoint is a nice red herring. Clean aerospace parts catalog.

String injection in vessel metrics with a keyword+space WAF. Calibrated at 0.8 — the easiest in the set. The WAF bypass is trivial (use comments instead of spaces). The app has good realism with vessels and manifests but the challenge offers minimal resistance.

Multi-layer discovery: JS file reveals API key and endpoint, then keyword stripping + space blocking WAF must be bypassed. The vtrack-api.js file with embedded credentials is realistic developer mistake. Bootstrap maritime theme is well-executed. Good challenge flow.

ORDER BY injection via POST JSON requiring a custom X-Aether-Internal header. The discovery of the internal header and analytics endpoint adds genuine enumeration steps. WAF blocks common keywords. The drone logistics hub theme is decent. Calibrated at 1.85.

The most defensively sophisticated vanilla challenge — multi-layer WAF (whitespace blocking + keyword blacklist + /**/ exception), custom header auth from a JS file, asset tag format validation, and blind boolean response. Players must chain: JS discovery → header auth → format constraint → WAF bypass → blind extraction. The highest complexity in the vanilla set.

String injection in a node status API with a keyword+whitespace regex WAF. The API is revealed in an HTML comment. Calibrated at 0.85 — trivially easy. The WAF bypass (use comments) is mechanical. One of the weakest vanilla entries.

Blind boolean SQLi in a telemetry status API with single-pass keyword + space stripping. The dark aerospace theme and maintenance portal discovery via footer link are well-done. The SELSELECTECT bypass is standard but the boolean-only response requires proper blind extraction technique.

String injection in a tracking API with a lowercase-only keyword WAF and space blocking. The bypass is trivial — use mixed case (sElEcT). The dark orbital theme is visually appealing and the tracking UI is well-designed, but the WAF provides almost no real defense.
